Get Started — Develop

Code Management Guidelines

Standards and practices for managing code throughout the development lifecycle — from branching to publishing.

Guidelines

01

Feature Main Branch (FMB)

Create a Feature Main Branch for centralized integration of feature branches, providing a stable baseline for testing and production.

Creation by Team Lead — Ensures a centralized and consistent approach to branch management.
Structured Development — Developers should follow the Code Branching Guidelines for structured development.
02

Frequent Commits

Encourage developers to make smaller, more frequent commits rather than large, infrequent ones. This facilitates easier code review and reduces conflicts.

Commit Messages — Developers should write informative and concise commit messages.
03

Pull Requests (PRs)

Require developers to create pull requests daily for code review before merging changes into the main branch.

04

Code Reviews

Make code reviews a mandatory part of the development process. Ensure that experienced team members review and provide constructive feedback on each pull request.

Reviewer Responsibility — Code reviewers play a crucial role in maintaining code quality; they should thoroughly review pull requests.
Review Schedule — PR reviews occur every 1:00 PM and 4:00 PM daily.
Merge Buffer — Ensure 5–10 minutes from the last approver before merging the branch.
05

FMB Regular Updates

All Feature Main Branches should be updated End of Business (EOB) daily.

06

Daily Pulls

Developers should pull changes from their respective Feature Main Branch daily to stay in sync with the latest code.

07

Publishing

Generate the publish copy from the Feature Main Branch for consistency.

Master Repository — Utilize the master repository for the version next in line for production deployment.
Separate Master Branch — Create a separate master branch in the Published Repository for subsequent FMBs.

Quick Reference

Practice Who Cadence
Create Feature Main Branch Team Lead Per version cycle
Commit changes Developer Frequently, small units
Create Pull Request Developer Daily
Code Review Reviewer 1:00 PM & 4:00 PM
Update FMB Team Lead End of Business (EOB)
Pull from FMB Developer Daily
Generate publish copy Team Lead Per release cycle
On this page
